pr.title: Matched in ftYs_SpecialN, mostly involving inlines
pr.author: Gelatart

summary: Matched several previously stubbed Yoshi SpecialN-related functions in src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialN.c, with most progress coming from carving out small inline helpers that reproduce shared command-variable, victim-gobj, callback, and motion-state setup behavior. The header was tightened for two functions by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete void signatures.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8012D128, replacing a decomp stub with logic that gets the Fighter from the gobj, calls ftCommon_8007D5D4, changes to motion state 0x15F using fp->cur_anim_frame and flags 0x0C4C509A, then installs callbacks through ftCommon_8007E2D0.
Implemented fn_8012D1AC similarly, but using ftCommon_8007D7FC, motion state 0x15A, and callbacks fn_8012CF7C/fn_8012CEE0/ftCo_800BBB8C.
Added small static helpers inlineA1 and inlineA0 for shared SpecialN/SpecialAirN command-variable reset and motion-state transition behavior.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialN1_1_Anim so it checks fp->cmd_vars[0] and transitions through inlineA0 to motion state 0x15E.
Added an inline Fighter_GetVictimGobj accessor returning fp->victim_gobj, used to match victim-gobj-dependent SpecialN behavior.

Added ftYs_SpecialN1_0_Anim_inline and implemented ftYs_SpecialN1_0_Anim as a wrapper around it; this path checks cmd_vars[0] and non-null victim_gobj before resetting command vars, changing to motion state 0x15D, and calling ftCommon_8007E2F4.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Anim so it checks fp->cmd_vars[0] and transitions through inlineA0 to motion state 0x163.

Implemented fn_8012E3B4 for Yoshi SpecialHi-related setup: calls ftCommon_8007D5D4, changes to motion state 0x16D with flags 0x0C4C5082, conditionally installs take_dmg_cb/death2_cb through fn_8012E3B4_inline, assigns accessory4_cb through qwe, then calls ftCommon_8007D468.
Updated ftYs_SpecialN.h prototypes for fn_8012E110 and fn_8012E3B4 from unknown signatures to concrete void callbacks taking HSD_GObj* or Fighter_GObj*.
smart_moves: Factored repeated command-variable clearing into inlineA1, making it reusable across multiple SpecialN animation handlers while preserving the matching-oriented inline shape.
Used inlineA0 to share the common conditional x1A60 check, motion-state transition, ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF00 call, and ftCommon_8007E2F4 call between grounded and aerial SpecialN follow-up paths.

Introduced a tiny Fighter_GetVictimGobj inline accessor rather than directly reading victim_gobj in the condition, indicating the original compiler shape likely required a separate inline for matching.
Kept ftYs_SpecialN1_0_Anim as a wrapper around ftYs_SpecialN1_0_Anim_inline, another sign that matching required reproducing an inline/helper boundary rather than only writing straightforward logic.
Split fn_8012E3B4 callback setup into fn_8012E3B4_inline and qwe helpers, suggesting careful reconstruction of the compiler's inline layout for callback assignments.
Improved header accuracy after matching by replacing broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete callback-style signatures.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action functions, small inline helpers can be necessary to match even when the visible behavior is simple assignments and motion-state calls.
Repeated cmd_vars[0]/cmd_vars[1] reset patterns should be considered candidates for extracted inline helpers when multiple action-state handlers share them.
When a condition involves a struct field such as victim_gobj, a tiny accessor inline may be required to reproduce the expected generated code.
Motion-state changes should preserve exact constants, argument order, float literals, and callback arguments; this PR used explicit msid constants such as 0x15D, 0x15E, 0x15F, 0x163, and 0x16D.
Once a formerly unknown function is matched, update its header prototype immediately so downstream files can rely on concrete types instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Callback fields such as accessory4_cb, take_dmg_cb, and death2_cb may need to be assigned in helper functions rather than inline in the parent function to match compiler output.
naming_conventions: Matched-but-still-unknown functions retained address-based names such as fn_8012D128, fn_8012D1AC, fn_8012E3B4, and fn_8012E110.
Action handlers followed the ftYs_SpecialN/SpecialAirN naming pattern, e.g. ftYs_SpecialN1_1_Anim, ftYs_SpecialN1_0_Anim, and ftYs_SpecialAirN1_2_Anim.

Temporary or matching-oriented helper names included inlineA0, inlineA1, ftYs_SpecialN1_0_Anim_inline, fn_8012E3B4_inline, and qwe; these names are evidence from the diff but are not descriptive final names.
The accessor name Fighter_GetVictimGobj follows a clear subsystem-style convention and directly describes returning fp->victim_gobj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted unused stack padding declarations `u8 _[8];` in fn_8012D128 and fn_8012D1AC, likely to match stack layout.
Used helper functions marked static or inline to reproduce expected inlining and call boundaries.
Kept explicit casts such as `(Fighter*) gobj->user_data` in ftCommon_8007E2D0 calls, preserving exact source shape around fighter data access.
Used exact unsigned motion-state flag constants such as `0x0C4C509AU`, `0x80012U`, and `0x0C4C5082U`.
Preserved exact float and integer literal forms in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, including `0.0f`, `1.0f`, integer `0`, integer `1`, and reuse of `fp->cur_anim_frame`.
Matched conditional callback setup by testing `fp->fv.ys.x2238 != NULL` before assigning `take_dmg_cb` and `death2_cb`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR context reports one issue comment, but no human text excerpt was included, so no substantive reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename inlineA0, inlineA1, qwe, or other temporary helpers in ftYs_SpecialN.c.
Search for other fighter action matches that use `u8 _[8];` stack padding around Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Compare other Yoshi SpecialN functions for the same cmd_vars reset and victim_gobj transition pattern.
Search for callback assignment patterns involving accessory4_cb, take_dmg_cb, and death2_cb in other fighter special moves.
Check whether Fighter_GetVictimGobj became a shared inline/helper elsewhere or stayed local to ftYs_SpecialN.c.
